    Governance

    Content Safety Integration

    Azure AI Content Safety integration patterns for filtering, moderation, and responsible AI governance in enterprise AI Landing Zones

    ​
    🛡️ Content Safety Integration

    ​
    Overview

    Azure AI Content Safety provides AI-powered content moderation capabilities that detect and filter harmful content across text and images. When integrated with Azure API Management in an AI Landing Zone, it becomes a critical Layer 4 (Security Fabric) component that enforces responsible AI policies at the gateway level.

    ​
    What is Azure AI Content Safety

    Azure AI Content Safety is a content moderation service that uses machine learning models to analyze content for potential harm across multiple categories:
    CategoryDescriptionUse Cases
    HateContent expressing hatred or discriminationCommunity platforms, social media
    SexualSexually explicit or suggestive contentCustomer service, public APIs
    ViolenceContent depicting or encouraging violenceGaming, content platforms
    Self-HarmContent encouraging self-injuryMental health, wellness apps

    ​
    Content Safety Capabilities

    Content Safety Analysis Pipeline:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   CONTENT SAFETY API                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌───────────────┐   ┌───────────────┐   ┌───────────────┐  │
│  │    INPUT      │   │    ANALYSIS   │   │    OUTPUT     │  │
│  │  (Text/Image) │──▶│  (ML Models)  │──▶│  (Severity)   │  │
│  └───────────────┘   └───────────────┘   └───────────────┘  │
│         │                    │                    │          │
│         │              ┌─────┴─────┐              │          │
│         │              │           │              │          │
│         │         Categories   Blocklists         │          │
│         │         (4 types)    (Custom)           │          │
│         │              │           │              │          │
│         └──────────────┴───────────┴──────────────┘          │
│                                                              │
│  Severity Levels: 0 (Safe) → 1 (Low) → 2 (Medium) → 3 (High)│
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Severity Levels and Thresholds

    Content Safety returns severity scores from 0 to 3:
    LevelScoreActionExample
    Safe0AllowNormal conversation
    Low1Log/FlagBorderline content
    Medium2Review/BlockPotentially harmful
    High3BlockClearly harmful

    ​
    Integration Architecture

    ​
    Content Safety as Layer 4 Component

    Content Safety integrates with the AI Landing Zone at multiple layers: 
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      LAYER 1: GOVERNANCE HUB                     │
│                    (Azure API Management)                        │
│                                                                  │
│   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│   │              CONTENT SAFETY INTEGRATION                 │    │
│   │                                                         │    │
│   │   ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────┐   │    │
│   │   │  Request   │───▶│  Content   │───▶│  Backend   │   │    │
│   │   │  Received  │    │  Check     │    │  Service   │   │    │
│   │   └────────────┘    └─────┬──────┘    └────────────┘   │    │
│   │                           │                           │    │
│   │                    ┌──────▼──────┐                      │    │
│   │                    │   LAYER 4   │                      │    │
│   │                    │   SECURITY  │                      │    │
│   │                    │   FABRIC    │                      │    │
│   │                    │             │                      │    │
│   │                    │  Content    │                      │    │
│   │                    │  Safety API │                      │    │
│   │                    └─────────────┘                      │    │
│   │                                                         │    │
│   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Request/Response Interception Patterns

    Content Safety can be applied at different stages:
    StagePatternUse Case
    Pre-processingCheck user input before AI processingPrevent harmful prompts
    Post-processingCheck AI response before deliveryFilter AI-generated content
    BothCheck both request and responseMaximum safety coverage

    ​
    Async vs Sync Moderation

    ModeLatencyUse CaseImplementation
    Synchronous50-200msReal-time APIs, chatAPIM policy blocking
    AsynchronousBackgroundBatch processing, loggingQueue-based analysis

    ​
    Implementation Patterns

    ​
    APIM Policy Integration

    ​
    Basic Content Safety Check

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Extract prompt from OpenAI request -->
    <set-variable name="prompt" value="@{
      JObject body = context.Request.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      return body?["messages"]?.Last?["content"]?.ToString() ?? "";
    }" />
    
    <!-- Send to Content Safety API -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="safety-check" timeout="5">
      <set-url>https://{{content-safety-name}}.cognitiveservices.azure.com/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2023-10-01</set-url>
      <set-method>POST</set-method>
      <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key">
        <value>{{content-safety-key}}</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="Content-Type">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("prompt", "")),
          new JProperty("categories", new JArray("Hate", "Sexual", "Violence", "SelfHarm")),
          new JProperty("blocklistNames", new JArray()),
          new JProperty("haltOnBlocklistHit", true)
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </send-request>
    
    <!-- Check safety results -->
    <set-variable name="safety-result" value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["safety-check"]).Body.As<JObject>())" />
    
    <choose>
      <when condition="@{
        var result = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null);
        if (result == null) return false;
        var categories = result["categoriesAnalysis"] as JArray;
        if (categories == null) return false;
        foreach (var cat in categories) {
          var severity = cat["severity"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0;
          if (severity >= 2) return true; // Medium or High
        }
        return false;
      }">
        <return-response>
          <set-status code="400" reason="Content Safety Violation" />
          <set-header name="Content-Type">
            <value>application/json</value>
          </set-header>
          <set-body>{
            "error": "Content violates safety policy",
            "violation": "@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null)?["categoriesAnalysis"]?.ToString())"
          }</set-body>
        </return-response>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Advanced Content Safety with Custom Categories

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Check content with custom thresholds -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="safety-check" timeout="5">
      <set-url>https://{{content-safety-name}}.cognitiveservices.azure.com/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2023-10-01</set-url>
      <set-method>POST</set-method>
      <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key">
        <value>{{content-safety-key}}</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="Content-Type">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("prompt", "")),
          new JProperty("categories", new JArray("Hate", "Sexual", "Violence", "SelfHarm")),
          new JProperty("blocklistNames", new JArray("profanity-list", "pii-patterns")),
          new JProperty("haltOnBlocklistHit", true),
          new JProperty("outputType", "FourSeverityLevels")
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </send-request>
    
    <set-variable name="safety-result" value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["safety-check"]).Body.As<JObject>())" />
    
    <!-- Log all safety checks for audit -->
    <log-to-eventhub logger-id="content-safety-logger">
      @{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
          new JProperty("type", "content-safety-check"),
          new JProperty("contractId", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("contract-id", "")),
          new JProperty("result", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null)?.ToString()),
          new JProperty("action", "pre-processing")
        ).ToString();
      }
    </log-to-eventhub>
    
    <!-- Block on Medium or High severity -->
    <choose>
      <when condition="@{
        var result = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null);
        if (result == null) return true; // Fail safe
        
        var categories = result["categoriesAnalysis"] as JArray;
        if (categories == null) return true;
        
        foreach (var cat in categories) {
          var category = cat["category"]?.ToString();
          var severity = cat["severity"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0;
          
          // Different thresholds per category
          if (category == "SelfHarm" && severity >= 1) return true; // Strict for self-harm
          if (category == "Violence" && severity >= 2) return true; // Medium+ for violence
          if (category == "Hate" && severity >= 2) return true;
          if (category == "Sexual" && severity >= 2) return true;
        }
        return false;
      }">
        <return-response>
          <set-status code="400" reason="Content Safety Violation" />
          <set-header name="Content-Type">
            <value>application/json</value>
          </set-header>
          <set-body>{
            "error": "Content violates safety policy",
            "code": "CONTENT_SAFETY_VIOLATION",
            "message": "The submitted content has been flagged by our safety system. Please review and modify your request."
          }</set-body>
        </return-response>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Post-Processing Response Filtering

    <policies>
  <outbound>
    <!-- Check AI response for safety -->
    <set-variable name="response-content" value="@{
      JObject body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      return body?["choices"]?.First?["message"]?.["content"]?.ToString() ?? "";
    }" />
    
    <!-- Send to Content Safety -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="response-safety" timeout="5">
      <set-url>https://{{content-safety-name}}.cognitiveservices.azure.com/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2023-10-01</set-url>
      <set-method>POST</set-method>
      <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key">
        <value>{{content-safety-key}}</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="Content-Type">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("response-content", "")),
          new JProperty("categories", new JArray("Hate", "Sexual", "Violence", "SelfHarm"))
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </send-request>
    
    <!-- Mask harmful content in response -->
    <choose>
      <when condition="@{
        var result = ((IResponse)context.Variables["response-safety"]).Body.As<JObject>();
        var categories = result["categoriesAnalysis"] as JArray;
        if (categories == null) return false;
        foreach (var cat in categories) {
          if (cat["severity"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0 >= 2) return true;
        }
        return false;
      }">
        <set-body>@{
          JObject body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
          body["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] = "[Content filtered by safety policy]";
          return body.ToString();
        }</set-body>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </outbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Configuration Examples

    ​
    Content Safety Resource Deployment (Bicep)

    param location string = resourceGroup().location
param contentSafetyName string
param tags object = {}

resource contentSafety 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts@2023-05-01' = {
  name: contentSafetyName
  location: location
  sku: {
    name: 'S0'
  }
  kind: 'ContentSafety'
  properties: {
    publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled'
    networkAcls: {
      defaultAction: 'Deny'
      ipRules: []
      virtualNetworkRules: [
        {
          id: subnetId
          ignoreMissingVnetServiceEndpoint: false
        }
      ]
    }
  }
  tags: tags
}

// Private endpoint for secure access
resource privateEndpoint 'Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints@2023-05-01' = {
  name: '${contentSafetyName}-pe'
  location: location
  properties: {
    subnet: {
      id: subnetId
    }
    privateLinkServiceConnections: [
      {
        name: '${contentSafetyName}-plsc'
        properties: {
          privateLinkServiceId: contentSafety.id
          groupIds: ['account']
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

// Store keys in Key Vault
module keyVaultSecret './keyvault-secret.bicep' = {
  name: 'content-safety-key'
  params: {
    keyVaultName: keyVaultName
    secretName: 'content-safety-key'
    secretValue: contentSafety.listKeys().key1
  }
}

    ​
    Terraform Configuration

    resource "azurerm_cognitive_account" "content_safety" {
  name                = var.content_safety_name
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  kind                = "ContentSafety"
  sku_name            = "S0"

  public_network_access_enabled = false

  network_acls {
    default_action = "Deny"
    
    virtual_network_rules {
      subnet_id = var.subnet_id
    }
  }

  tags = var.tags
}

# Private endpoint
resource "azurerm_private_endpoint" "content_safety" {
  name                = "${var.content_safety_name}-pe"
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  subnet_id           = var.subnet_id

  private_service_connection {
    name                           = "${var.content_safety_name}-plsc"
    private_connection_resource_id = azurerm_cognitive_account.content_safety.id
    is_manual_connection           = false
    subresource_names              = ["account"]
  }
}

# Store key in Key Vault
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "content_safety_key" {
  name         = "content-safety-key"
  value        = azurerm_cognitive_account.content_safety.primary_access_key
  key_vault_id = var.key_vault_id
}

    ​
    API Integration Code Sample

    using Azure;
using Azure.AI.ContentSafety;

public class ContentSafetyService
{
    private readonly ContentSafetyClient _client;
    
    public ContentSafetyService(string endpoint, string key)
    {
        _client = new ContentSafetyClient(
            new Uri(endpoint), 
            new AzureKeyCredential(key));
    }
    
    public async Task<SafetyResult> AnalyzeTextAsync(string text)
    {
        var request = new AnalyzeTextOptions(text);
        
        // Analyze all categories
        request.Categories.Add(TextCategory.Hate);
        request.Categories.Add(TextCategory.Sexual);
        request.Categories.Add(TextCategory.Violence);
        request.Categories.Add(TextCategory.SelfHarm);
        
        var response = await _client.AnalyzeTextAsync(request);
        
        return new SafetyResult
        {
            IsSafe = response.Value.CategoriesAnalysis.All(
                c => c.Severity < 2),
            Categories = response.Value.CategoriesAnalysis.Select(
                c => new CategoryResult
                {
                    Category = c.Category.ToString(),
                    Severity = c.Severity ?? 0
                }).ToList()
        };
    }
}

    ​
    Multi-Language Support

    Content Safety supports content in multiple languages:
    LanguageCodeSupport Level
    EnglishenFull
    SpanishesFull
    FrenchfrFull
    GermandeFull
    ChinesezhFull
    JapanesejaFull
    PortugueseptFull
    ItalianitFull
    ArabicarPartial
    HindihiPartial
    <!-- Language-aware content check -->
<set-body>@{
  return new JObject(
    new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("prompt", "")),
    new JProperty("categories", new JArray("Hate", "Sexual", "Violence", "SelfHarm")),
    new JProperty("language", context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Accept-Language", "en").Split(',')[0])
  ).ToString();
}</set-body>

    ​
    Policy Enforcement

    ​
    Block vs Flag Decisions

    ActionThresholdUse CaseImplementation
    BlockSeverity ≥ 2Production APIs, strict complianceReturn 400 error
    FlagSeverity ≥ 1Logging, review queuesLog and allow
    AllowSeverity = 0All safe contentPass through

    ​
    Custom Business Logic

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <choose>
      <!-- Healthcare: Extra strict on self-harm -->
      <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("contract-industry", "") == "healthcare")">
        <set-variable name="self-harm-threshold" value="1" />
      </when>
      <!-- Gaming: Standard thresholds -->
      <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("contract-industry", "") == "gaming")">
        <set-variable name="self-harm-threshold" value="2" />
      </when>
      <!-- Default: Balanced approach -->
      <otherwise>
        <set-variable name="self-harm-threshold" value="2" />
      </otherwise>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Audit Logging for Flagged Content

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- After safety check -->
    <choose>
      <when condition="@{
        var result = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null);
        if (result == null) return false;
        var categories = result["categoriesAnalysis"] as JArray;
        if (categories == null) return false;
        return categories.Any(c => c["severity"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0 >= 1);
      }">
        <log-to-eventhub logger-id="flagged-content-logger">
          @{
            return new JObject(
              new JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
              new JProperty("type", "flagged-content"),
              new JProperty("contractId", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("contract-id", "")),
              new JProperty("userId", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("user-id", "")),
              new JProperty("safetyResult", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("safety-result", null)?.ToString()),
              new JProperty("action", "flagged-for-review"),
              new JProperty("clientIp", context.Request.IpAddress)
            ).ToString();
          }
        </log-to-eventhub>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Performance Considerations

    ​
    Latency Impact

    ConfigurationTypical LatencyImpact
    Simple text check50-100msMinimal
    Text + blocklists100-150msLow
    Image analysis500-2000msSignificant
    Multiple callsAdditiveConsider batching

    ​
    Batching Strategies

    // Batch multiple content checks
public async Task<BatchResult> AnalyzeBatchAsync(List<string> texts)
{
    var tasks = texts.Select(async text =>
    {
        var result = await AnalyzeTextAsync(text);
        return new { Text = text, Result = result };
    });
    
    var results = await Task.WhenAll(tasks);
    return new BatchResult { Results = results.ToList() };
}

    ​
    Caching Recommendations

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Cache safety results for repeated content -->
    <cache-lookup-value key="safety-@((context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("prompt", "")).GetHashCode())" 
                        variable-name="cached-safety" 
                        default-value="null" />
    
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("cached-safety", null) == null)">
        <!-- Perform safety check -->
        <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="safety-check" timeout="5">
          <!-- ... API call ... -->
        </send-request>
        
        <!-- Cache result for 5 minutes -->
        <cache-store-value key="safety-@((context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("prompt", "")).GetHashCode())" 
                           value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["safety-check"]).Body.As<JObject>().ToString())"
                           duration="300" />
      </when>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Best Practices

    ​
    Threshold Tuning

    1. Start Conservative: Begin with severity ≥ 2 blocking
    2. Monitor False Positives: Track blocked requests and user complaints
    3. Industry-Specific: Adjust thresholds based on use case
    4. Gradual Relaxation: Slowly lower thresholds as confidence increases

    ​
    False Positive Handling

    StrategyImplementation
    Appeal ProcessAllow users to request manual review
    Allow ListsMaintain lists of approved terms/phrases
    Context AwarenessConsider conversation context in decisions
    Feedback LoopReport false positives to improve models

    ​
    Human Review Workflows

    Flagged Content Review Process:

┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│   Content    │───▶│   Flagged    │───▶│   Review     │───▶│   Decision   │
│   Flagged    │    │   Queue      │    │   Team       │    │   Made       │
└──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘
                                                           │
                              ┌────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┐
                              │                            │                            │
                              ▼                            ▼                            ▼
                        ┌──────────┐                ┌──────────┐                ┌──────────┐
                        │  Allow   │                │  Block   │                │  Escalate│
                        │  List    │                │  List    │                │          │
                        └──────────┘                └──────────┘                └──────────┘

    ​
    Content Safety Studio Usage

    The Content Safety Studio provides a web-based interface for:
    FeaturePurpose
    Interactive TestingTest content against policies
    Threshold TuningAdjust severity thresholds visually
    Blocklist ManagementCreate and manage custom blocklists
    Analytics DashboardView moderation statistics
    Feedback IntegrationSubmit false positives for model improvement

    ​
    Integration with Layer 4 Security Fabric

    Content Safety works alongside other Layer 4 components: 
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    LAYER 4: SECURITY FABRIC                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│   ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐         │
│   │  Content    │  │  Microsoft  │  │  Microsoft  │         │
│   │  Safety     │  │  Defender   │  │  Purview    │         │
│   │  (Content)  │  │  (Threats)  │  │  (Data)     │         │
│   └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘         │
│          │                │                │                │
│          └────────────────┼────────────────┘                │
│                           │                                 │
│                    ┌──────▼──────┐                          │
│                    │   Unified   │                          │
│                    │   Response  │                          │
│                    └─────────────┘                          │
│                                                              │
│   Integration Points:                                        │
│   • Defender: Threat context for jailbreak detection         │
│   • Purview: PII detection alongside content safety          │
│   • Entra: User context for safety decisions                 │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
